title: Notification service dead letter queue overflowing due to malformed webhook payload

task.summary: Detect, localize, and repair a dead letter queue overflow in the webhook dispatcher caused by a consistently malformed outbound payload.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the DLQ overflow, localize the fault to the payload serialization path producing invalid JSON for a specific event type, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This local ops task is synthetic but uses realistic incident framing.

visible_context.issue_text: Webhook delivery success rate has dropped from 99.8% to 61%. The dead letter queue depth is growing rapidly. Downstream partners are reporting missing notifications. Retries are failing consistently for a specific event type.
error_logs: webhook-dispatcher: delivery failed for event_type=subscription.renewed — HTTP 400 from endpoint
webhook-dispatcher: retry 3/3 exhausted — moving to DLQ
partner-endpoint: 400 Bad Request — invalid JSON: unexpected token at position 214
serializer: subscription.renewed payload contains undefined field 'renewal_price' (type=NaN)
reproduction_steps: Inspect the DLQ contents and identify the common event type.
Examine the serialized payload for subscription.renewed events.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair sanitizes the renewal_price field before serialization (coerce NaN to null or a default value) and adds payload validation before dispatch.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident where a NaN value in a webhook payload causes consistent 400 rejections, exhausting retries and overflowing the DLQ.

notes: Synthetic local DLQ overflow ops task.
